idea-plugin.name: RepoBuddy
idea-plugin.description:
RepoBuddy: Run, Trace, and Inspect Spring Data Repositories in IntelliJ IDEA

RepoBuddy gives Spring Boot teams a faster way to understand and validate Spring Data JPA behavior directly inside IntelliJ IDEA. Execute repository methods from the editor, inspect generated SQL, review returned data, and trace endpoint-to-repository flows without leaving the IDE.

Instead of switching between tests, REST tools, logs, and debugger sessions, RepoBuddy keeps repository execution and analysis in one focused workflow for everyday development and debugging.

Core capabilities

  • Repository runner from the editor - launch repository methods directly from a gutter action.
  • Live execution against the real application - run against your active Spring Boot runtime, not a simulated environment.
  • Structured parameter forms - generate input controls based on repository method parameter types.
  • Random sample data generation - populate parameters quickly with sensible generated values.
  • SQL inspection - view the exact Hibernate SQL statements triggered by execution.
  • Formatted result viewer - inspect returned JSON, execution time, and runtime errors in one place.
  • Repository Usage dashboard - review repository methods, call counts, unused entries, and operation types across the project.
  • Call Chain Tracer - trace controller and service flows down to repository access with transaction-aware context.

Why RepoBuddy

  • Reduce the edit-run-debug cycle when validating persistence logic.
  • See repository behavior in the real runtime with the real SQL output.
  • Understand usage and impact before refactoring repository-heavy code.
  • Keep execution, tracing, and inspection inside IntelliJ IDEA.

Typical workflow

  1. Start your Spring Boot application from IntelliJ IDEA.
  2. Open a Spring Data repository interface.
  3. Trigger RepoBuddy from the repository method gutter action.
  4. Enter values or generate sample input.
  5. Review SQL logs, returned data, execution time, and errors in one popup.

Best suited for

  • Spring Boot developers debugging JPA and repository behavior.
  • Teams working with complex queries, filtering, pagination, and transactional flows.
  • Developers reviewing or refactoring data-access codebases.

Requirements

  • IntelliJ IDEA 2024.1 or later.
  • Java 17 or later.
  • Spring Boot with Spring Data JPA.
  • An application launched from IntelliJ IDEA.

Resources

  • Issues: GitHub Issues
  • Source code: github.com/ELglaly/RepoBuddy
  • Website: elglaly.github.io/Sherif-Elglaly
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.3 - UI Polish and Marketplace Presentation

  • Refined the Repository Usage table with cleaner badges, improved typography, and a more polished layout.
  • Redesigned the Call Chain Tracer tree and the repository runner popup for a more professional, readable presentation.
  • Updated the Marketplace plugin description and metadata wording for a clearer, stronger overview page.

1.0.2 - Repository Filtering & Endpoint Search

  • Current File filter - new toggle in the Repository Usage tab. Open any repository file (e.g. UserRepository.java) and click Current File to scope the table to that repository's methods instantly. The table auto-populates on panel open so the filter is ready without a manual Refresh.
  • Endpoint search in Call Chain Tracer - live filter field above the endpoint dropdown. Type an HTTP verb (e.g. POST) or a path fragment (e.g. /users) and the dropdown narrows in real time; the matching list opens automatically as you type.
  • Full API path display - endpoint paths now include the controller-level @RequestMapping class prefix. Previously only the method-level path was shown (e.g. /{id}); now the complete URL is shown (e.g. /api/v1/users/{id}).
  • Cleaner endpoint combo display - the endpoint selector now shows only the HTTP verb and path ([GET] /api/users) without the controller class name and method signature, reducing visual noise.

1.0.1 - Stability & Build Improvements

  • Enhanced build configuration and random data generation strategies
  • Updated settings and plugin files for improved documentation and versioning

1.0.0 - Initial Release

  • Run gutter actions on Spring Data repository methods
  • Live execution via embedded Java agent
  • SQL capture via Hibernate StatementInspector
  • Smart parameter form with per-type widgets
  • Random data generation with field-level and generate-all actions
  • Pageable and enum-aware parameter handling
  • Call Chain Tracer with summary insights and source navigation
  • Repository Usage table with filtering and export support
  • Automatic run-configuration patching on project open
  • Theme-aware UI for light and dark IntelliJ themes

action.text: Trace Repository Calls for This API
action.description: Trace all repository methods reachable from this Spring endpoint
